definition_plain: A sandbox is an isolated execution environment where an AI agent can run code or use tools without affecting production systems. If the agent makes a mistake — deleting files, running an infinite loop, calling a dangerous API — the damage stays contained within the sandbox. It's like giving a new employee a test account instead of production credentials: they can experiment freely without risk.

scenario.meeting_line: The agent needs to run user-submitted code — we're sandboxing it in E2B so a rogue script can't access our infra.
scenario.smart_followup: What's the sandbox timeout? If we don't cap execution time, someone could mine crypto in our sandbox instances.

quiz_question.question: An AI coding agent needs to execute arbitrary Python scripts submitted by users. To prevent a malicious script from accessing the company's database or file system, the team runs all code in an isolated container with no network access. What is this container called?
options: B) A sandbox — isolated execution environment preventing damage to production
quiz_question.explanation: Sandboxes isolate code execution so that mistakes or malicious actions can't affect production systems or data.
